## Input

Signal from 2026-06-14 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-260):
- 19 blocked issues, 9 of which are blocked specifically waiting on admin credentials:
  - DEV-108: GH PAT for newtechkw org + codegraph install + Pulse v3 approval (escalation_deadline=2026-06-14 TODAY, waiting_on present since 2026-06-07)
  - DEV-93: GitHub PAT (RW + RO) for Build Squad agents
  - DEV-96: CONTEXT7_API_KEY for code agents
  - DEV-97: Sentry credentials for deployment/QA/security agents
  - DEV-126: FIGMA_TOKEN for figma-to-ux-spec skill
  - DEV-169: CEO desktop daemon offline (urgent, since 2026-05-31)
  - DEV-197: ADR-002 App Hub Model blockers (re-escalation, since 2026-06-01)
  - DEV-39 + DEV-40: adminnewtech/attendance-app GitHub repo not provisioned
- No workspace memory entry exists that standardises: (a) which credentials are outstanding, (b) when each was first requested, (c) re-escalation cadence (current pattern: silent drift for weeks).

## Expected Output

A set of memory entries (one per outstanding admin blocker) that the CEO/autopilot can load on each run to:
1. Know which credentials are still missing without re-scanning all blocked issues.
2. Know when each was first requested and when to re-escalate.
3. Know the escalation contact (admin@newtechkw.com) and the issue to comment on.

Concrete deliverables:
- Create/update workspace memory entries documenting the outstanding blockers using available memory tooling (note in comment if a `multica memory` command exists; otherwise document the default fact-capture approach used in CLAUDE.md).
- If no multica memory CLI exists, post a structured `##Admin Blockers Summary` comment on the Workspace Operations Dialog (DEV-36 is excluded per rules — find an appropriate ops issue or post to the parent of DEV-108) so the pattern survives across agent runs.
- Recommended memory keys per blocker: `credential_name`, `first_requested_at`, `escalation_deadline`, `waiting_on_issue`, `re_escalation_interval_days`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 9 admin-blocked issues captured with: credential name, first-request date, deadline/urgency
- [ ] Re-escalation cadence defined: suggest every 7 days for medium-priority, every 3 days for urgent (DEV-169)
- [ ] Memory accessible to future CEO runs (either workspace memory or pinned ops comment)
- [ ] DEV-108 escalation_deadline=2026-06-14 flagged as overdue → post a re-escalation note on DEV-108
- [ ] No secrets or credential values stored — only the names/types of missing credentials

## Dependencies

- DEV-108 (parent blocker, today's deadline): issue ID `4850d6c3-e432-4b72-9add-1d0e27a7cfe5`
- DEV-169 (urgent runtime offline): issue ID `4ba6e4f2-a285-4048-add8-389d8a5f5dbc`
- No external blockers for capture; admin must supply credentials to actually unblock

## Risk

Low. Read-only scan + memory write. No side effects on issues except adding a re-escalation comment on DEV-108 (which is appropriate and explicitly needed — deadline passed today).

## Definition of Done

- Memory entries or structured comment created for all 9 admin blockers
- Re-escalation note posted on DEV-108 (overdue today)
- DEV-169 urgent runtime blocker flagged as top priority for admin
- Result comment on this issue listing what was captured
issues.title: Workspace Upgrade Action — Capture admin-escalation blockers as memory defaults

## SECURITY REVIEW: supabase-mcp-setup import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-242](mention://issue/46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/supabase/mcp (v0.8.2)
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- HTTP MCP server at `https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp` — no local process
- Self-hosted option: `http://localhost:54321/mcp` (Supabase CLI)
- Requires: Supabase personal access token OR OAuth 2.1 flow
- Client-side config: `.mcp.json` entry pointing to the MCP URL

### Required scopes
- **PAT path:** Personal access token with `supabase:mcp` scope (full project access by default)
- **OAuth path:** `supabase:mcp` OAuth scope
- **Write capabilities (default, not read_only):** execute arbitrary SQL, create/drop tables, create users, manage auth, deploy edge functions
- **Read-only mode (recommended):** `?read_only=true` restricts to SELECT and read operations
- **Project scoping:** `?project_ref=<ref>` limits to one project

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Token handling:** Confirm the skill template stores PATs only as env vars (never in `.mcp.json` or committed files)
2. **Default posture:** Verify skill enforces `read_only=true` as the documented default and requires explicit user approval to remove
3. **Scope documentation:** Confirm that write-mode removal requires workspace-owner approval per Security Rules (all write paths to external systems)
4. **Token revocation path:** Verify the skill documents how to revoke a PAT if compromised
5. **Self-hosted vs cloud:** Confirm self-hosted local MCP is lower risk than cloud MCP (no external data transmission)
6. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if skill template defaults to read-only, no PAT in config files, revocation path documented
- `decision=yellow` if write-mode default but mitigations documented (downgrade to explicit warning in skill)
- `decision=red` if PAT leakage risk or no scoping mechanism

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`
issues.title: SECURITY REVIEW: supabase-mcp-setup import

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-06-15 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Daily Command Center Digest — 2026-06-15

## Input

Signal from 2026-06-14 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-260):
- 35 of 47 in_review issues are stale >48h. These are Daily Digest (DEV-254, DEV-252, ...) and Factory Health Audit (DEV-253, DEV-251, ...) autopilot issues.
- The quality-gate-manager (QGM) instructions contain an "Autopilot issue fast-path" that should close these automatically, but the queue shows this path is not consistently firing.
- Matching criteria A: assignee is ceo or workspace-operations-manager. Criteria B: title starts with "Daily Command Center Digest —", "Factory Health Audit —", "Daily Factory Health Audit —", or "Workspace Upgrade Report —" OR description contains "Mode=create_issue" or "Autopilot run triggered".
- Current QGM instructions say: if comment count ≥ 1 → close as done; if comment count = 0 → cancel as anomaly.
- Pattern not consistently observed: DEV-255 (Factory Health Audit 2026-06-13) has metadata={}, meaning QGM may not have run the fast-path correctly.

## Expected Output

A prompt patch to quality-gate-manager instructions that:
1. Strengthens the fast-path trigger: adds explicit examples of matching title patterns including "Daily Factory Health Audit —" (hyphen variant observed in DEV-251).
2. Adds a priority note: "Process autopilot fast-path FIRST before any standard quality gate work to prevent queue accumulation."
3. Adds a sweep clause: "On each QGM run, sweep all in_review issues — not just the one that triggered this run — and apply the fast-path to all autopilot-pattern issues found in a single pass."
4. Result: a concrete `multica agent update <qgm-id> --instructions "<patched text>"` command, or a description of the exact text delta to insert.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Patched instructions text written and reviewed for correctness
- [ ] Title pattern list is exhaustive: "Daily Command Center Digest —", "Factory Health Audit —", "Daily Factory Health Audit —", "Workspace Upgrade Report —"
- [ ] Sweep-all clause is present (not just the triggering issue)
- [ ] Priority ordering: fast-path before standard gate
- [ ] Instructions updated on quality-gate-manager via multica CLI
- [ ] Comment on this issue with: diff of changed text + expected outcome

## Dependencies

- quality-gate-manager agent ID: `d87c831d-bd4d-459f-9672-d633e238c44f`
- Read current QGM instructions first: `multica agent get d87c831d-bd4d-459f-9672-d633e238c44f --output json`
- No external blockers

## Risk

Medium. Modifying QGM instructions affects all in_review quality gates. Risk: too-broad fast-path accidentally closes real shippable work. Mitigation: fast-path only applies when BOTH criteria A (autopilot assignee) AND B (title/description pattern) match simultaneously.

## Definition of Done

- QGM instructions updated
- Fast-path sweep clause present
- Stale in_review count drops by ≥10 on the next QGM run
- Result comment on this issue
issues.title: Workspace Upgrade Action — Fix QGM prompt to enforce autopilot fast-path for stale in_review

## SECURITY REVIEW: sandbox-process-runner import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-241](mention://issue/14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- npm package: `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` (global install)
- Installs `srt` CLI binary
- Wraps target process with macOS `sandbox-exec` or Linux `bubblewrap`
- Intercepts network via proxy-based filtering (man-in-the-middle for TLS inspection)
- No persistent daemon; process lifecycle tied to the sandboxed command

### Required scopes
- Root/sudo NOT required on Linux (bubblewrap is user-namespaces based)
- macOS: uses `sandbox-exec` (built-in macOS API, no elevated privileges)
- Network proxy: runs on loopback, intercepts outbound HTTP/HTTPS via LD_PRELOAD or system proxy env vars

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Supply chain:** Confirm `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` is the official Anthropic package (not a typosquat)
2. **TLS proxy risk:** The package performs MITM on TLS to enforce network restrictions — verify the MITM key is ephemeral and not exported
3. **Active CVEs this week:** TLS terminate host header spoofing fix (PR #296, 2026-06-05), shell-quote bump to 1.8.4 (PR #301), leaf cert validity tightened (85a66578) — confirm all are in v0.x.y on npm
4. **Scope creep:** Verify `srt` cannot be used to escalate privileges or escape to the parent process namespace
5. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits commercial use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if supply chain confirmed, MITM key ephemeral, no privilege escalation path
- `decision=red` if supply chain risk or unpatched CVE

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`
issues.title: SECURITY REVIEW: sandbox-process-runner import

## Task

Assign the newly published aegis-method-pack skill bundle to all relevant build and coding agents in the workspace.

## Skills to Assign

The following 6 skills were published on 2026-06-15 (source: https://github.com/GanyuanRan/Aegis, MIT, v2.1.5):

| Skill ID | Name | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| f73a526a-27a8-4036-8825-8afd9ba0328b | `aegis-method-pack` | Meta routing skill — trigger this for any build/debug/review task |
| 53d7b1a8-1aa4-48b5-bf17-7d5909512e8f | `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallel sub-agent orchestration |
| 1d56149b-3887-4322-abc3-4c0e1dfd75bd | `systematic-debugging` | Root-cause-first debugging |
| a414b918-4992-4a14-9f8b-ff665304efc5 | `test-driven-development` | RED→GREEN→REFACTOR TDD gates |
| 5be6de74-3459-4343-8b3d-eb5bf8e54102 | `verification-before-completion` | Evidence-backed completion verification |
| caa47a02-0d68-4ef4-b8a2-87d73a1d3176 | `establishing-project-context` | Domain glossary / CONTEXT.md setup |

## Suggested Target Agents

Assign at minimum to all agents that:
- Implement features (build squad agents)
- Debug or fix bugs
- Review code or create PRs
- Run QA or verification tasks

Priority: `aegis-method-pack` + `verification-before-completion` + `systematic-debugging` are the highest-value assignments for any coding agent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aegis-method-pack` assigned to all build/coding agents
- [ ] `verification-before-completion` assigned to all agents that make completion claims
- [ ] `systematic-debugging` assigned to agents that debug or fix bugs

issues.description:
# Skill: weekly-status-email

## Purpose

Generate a structured weekly status email summarizing what the team shipped, current blockers, and priorities for the coming week. Designed to be invoked by any team member or agent at the end of each work week or sprint, requiring no manual formatting.

## Trigger

Invoke when a team member or manager asks for a weekly status update email (e.g. "send the weekly update", "draft the status email for this week", "write the end-of-sprint email"). Also suitable for automation on a recurring Friday/end-of-sprint schedule.

## Required Input

- **project** — The project name or ID to query for closed issues (e.g. `DEV`, `88c09466-8a25-419d-b28e-d3d5973197c4`).
- **week_start** — ISO date for the start of the reporting period (e.g. `2026-06-09`).
- **week_end** — ISO date for the end of the reporting period (e.g. `2026-06-15`).
- **recipient** — (Optional) Name of the recipient or audience. Defaults to "Team" if not provided.

## Steps

1. Run `multica issue list --project <project> --status done --output json` (or equivalent) to retrieve all issues closed during the `week_start`–`week_end` period.
2. Filter to issues whose `updated_at` or `done_at` falls within the date range.
3. Extract human-readable titles only — strip internal IDs, URLs, and technical identifiers from the output.
4. Query for any issues currently in `blocked` status within the same project to populate the Blockers section.
5. Query for issues in `todo` or `in_progress` status to determine next week's priorities (top 5 by priority field).
6. Compose the email:
   - **Subject:** `Weekly Status Update — Week of <week_start>`
   - **Greeting:** `Hi <recipient>,`
   - **What we shipped:** Bullet list of closed issue titles (plain language, no IDs).
   - **Blockers:** Bullet list of blocked issue titles + one-line blocker reason if available in the issue description. If none, write "No active blockers."
   - **Next week:** Top 5 priority issue titles from `todo`/`in_progress`.
   - **Sign-off:** `Best, [Agent / Team]`
7. Output the email as plain text (no markdown headers, no internal URLs, no issue identifiers).
8. Present the draft to the invoker for approval before sending (if a send action is available).

## Output

A plain-text email body ready to send or paste:
- Subject line
- Greeting
- "What we shipped" section (bullet list, human-readable titles only)
- "Blockers" section (bullet list or "No active blockers")
- "Next week" section (up to 5 priorities)
- Sign-off line

Maximum length: 400 words. Tone: professional, concise, readable by non-technical stakeholders.

## Quality Checklist

- [ ] All four required inputs are present or defaulted before running.
- [ ] Closed issues are filtered to the exact `week_start`–`week_end` window.
- [ ] No internal issue IDs, UUIDs, or URLs appear in the email body.
- [ ] Blockers section is present (even if it says "No active blockers").
- [ ] Next week section lists ≤ 5 items.
- [ ] Email is plain text with no markdown headers or code blocks.
- [ ] Draft is presented for human approval before any send action.

## Failure Handling

- **No closed issues found for the period:** Write "No issues were closed this week." in the shipped section and note it may mean the date range is wrong — ask the invoker to confirm.
- **Project not found or access denied:** Report the error, ask the invoker to confirm the project name/ID, and retry.
- **Blocker query fails:** Omit the blockers section and note it in the email draft as "Blocker data unavailable — check manually."
- **Draft exceeds 400 words:** Summarize each bullet to ≤ 15 words and truncate to top 7 shipped items.

## Improvement Rule

Review after 5 uses. Refine if:
- Recipients report the email is too long or technical.
- Closed-issue filtering misses or double-counts items (adjust date filter logic).
- A scheduling autopilot is set up — add a step to post the draft to a Slack channel automatically.
- Multica adds a native reporting export (retire the manual query steps).

## Context

Benchmark action from DEV-268: WorkspaceAgentSkillPersonalization. Replit Agent and Cursor have shipped workspace-level personalization UIs this week, letting non-technical users define reusable agent behaviors without CLI/CLAUDE.md knowledge. We need to close that gap.

## Task

Design and publish a new skill: **`user-skill-creation-wizard`**

This skill is a guided conversation flow that any workspace member (non-technical) can invoke to define a mini-skill. No CLAUDE.md authoring knowledge required.

## Required Skill Anatomy (every field is mandatory)

- **Name:** `user-skill-creation-wizard`
- **Purpose:** Guide any workspace member through a 4-step conversation to define a mini-skill, then produce a well-formed SKILL.md draft and open a Skill Library review issue.
- **Trigger:** When a workspace member wants to define a reusable agent behavior but lacks skill-authoring knowledge.
- **Required Input:** User must supply answers to 4 wizard questions (see Steps below).
- **Steps:**
  1. Ask for skill name and one-line description. Validate: name must be kebab-case, description ≤ 120 chars.
  2. Ask for trigger condition in plain language (when should the agent invoke this skill?).
  3. Ask what the agent should do: behavior, output format, constraints. Provide an example to anchor the user.
  4. Ask for optional input fields the user must supply when invoking. Offer to skip if none.
  5. Run `multica skill list` (if available) to check for naming collisions; rename if conflict found.
  6. Apply the workspace quality checklist: purpose ✓, trigger ✓, required input ✓, steps ✓, output ✓, quality checklist ✓, failure handling ✓.
  7. Generate SKILL.md content from the collected answers.
  8. Create an issue in `08 - Skill Library` (project `88c09466-8a25-419d-b28e-d3d5973197c4`) titled `[Wizard Draft] <skill-name>` with the SKILL.md as the description and status `todo`, assignee: skill-builder.
  9. Reply to the user with a link to the new issue and the generated SKILL.md preview.
- **Output:** SKILL.md file content + a new `08 - Skill Library` issue in `todo` status ready for agent review.
- **Quality checklist:** All 7 SKILL.md sections present; trigger condition is specific enough to auto-detect; steps are actionable without ambiguity; output is concrete; naming collision checked.
- **Failure handling:** If user provides vague behavior description, re-ask with a concrete example. If naming collision found, suggest 2-3 alternatives before re-asking.
- **Improvement rule:** Update when Multica adds a skill import UI or when 3+ wizard-generated skills are found to have low quality scores.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `user-skill-creation-wizard` skill file created and importable in the workspace
- [ ] Skill passes the 7-section workspace quality checklist
- [ ] Test case documented: invoke the wizard for a "weekly status email template" mini-skill and produce a ready-for-review SKILL.md
- [ ] A `08 - Skill Library` issue created for the test case output
- [ ] No hardcoded squad/assignee assumptions in the wizard (squad-agnostic)
- [ ] Skill extraction note added as a comment on DEV-268

## Definition of Done

- Skill published and importable
- At least one end-to-end test run documented (wizard invoked → SKILL.md produced → Skill Library review issue created)
- Comment posted on parent issue DEV-268 with summary and links
- No secrets exposed

issues.description:
## Input

**Pattern observed:** WorkspaceAgentSkillPersonalization — users defining persistent custom instructions and named reusable skills at the workspace level, applied by the AI agent across all projects automatically.

**Vendors shipping this pattern this week:**
- **Replit Agent (Jun 10/12):** Workspace Settings UI lets any user write custom instructions that Agent applies globally across all projects, plus define named reusable "skills" that Agent can invoke. Ships as a self-serve settings panel — no CLAUDE.md or CLI knowledge required.
- **Cursor (Jun 11):** Auto-review profiling builds workspace-level behavioral context for agent governance, extending the pattern to risk/approval personalization.

**The gap in Multica today:** Our skill system is powerful but requires a technical agent (or the workspace owner) to author skills as structured CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md files and publish them via CLI. Non-technical workspace members cannot define or publish skills without agent mediation. Replit's version closes this gap with a UI-first flow.

**Scoring:** frequency=2/6 | impact=4 | effort=S | score=8 (highest this sweep)

---

## Expected Output

A new skill in the Skill Library (`08 - Skill Library`):

**Name:** `user-skill-creation-wizard`

**What it produces:** A guided conversation flow that any workspace member (non-technical) can invoke to define a mini-skill. The wizard collects:
1. Skill name and one-line description
2. When to use it (trigger condition, in plain language)
3. What the agent should do (behavior, output format, constraints)
4. Optional: input fields the user must supply when invoking

At the end of the conversation, the wizard generates a well-formed SKILL.md file and creates an issue in `08 - Skill Library` to formalize the skill for agent review before publishing. The wizard itself requires no CLAUDE.md knowledge from the user.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Wizard skill is published in `08 - Skill Library` and importable
- [ ] Any workspace member can invoke the wizard via a Multica issue comment or direct invocation without prior skill-authoring knowledge
- [ ] Wizard produces a SKILL.md draft that passes the workspace's skill quality checklist (purpose, trigger, required input, steps, output, quality checklist, failure handling)
- [ ] A test case: a non-technical user defines a "weekly status email template" mini-skill via the wizard, and the output SKILL.md is ready for agent review
- [ ] No hardcoded assumptions about which squad reviews the skill (the wizard should be squad-agnostic)

---

## Dependencies

- `08 - Skill Library` project must be accessible (assumed yes)
- Skill creation rules (3-times rule, SKILL.md format) from workspace CLAUDE.md must be codified as the wizard's internal rubric
- Optionally: a UI prompt for the Skill Factory Squad to review and publish the wizard's output

---

## Risk

- **Low:** Wizard generates a SKILL.md that doesn't meet the quality bar → mitigated by including the quality checklist as the wizard's final validation step before producing output
- **Medium:** Non-technical users describe vague behaviors that produce low-quality skills → mitigated by structured question flow with examples at each step
- **Low:** Skill naming collisions with existing skills → wizard should call `multica skill list` to check for duplicates before finalizing

---

## Definition of Done

- Skill published and importable
- At least one end-to-end test run documented in the skill's issue (wizard invoked → SKILL.md produced → Skill Factory review issue created)
- Skill extraction note added to this sweep issue (DEV-267)
- No secrets exposed

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.

    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```

- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">

- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

issues.description:
Weekly GitHub Research Sweep — keeps the workspace's skill ecosystem fresh. Runs every Monday 10:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Mode=create_issue (project: 09 - Research Intelligence, id e977793d-b825-448a-a3f1-bb8f59cc1266).

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the up-to-3 sub-issues for skill-builder dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention the CEO (d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) in any comment — assignment is the trigger.
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up or thank-you. Only mention an agent on the dispatch sub-issues, and only if assignment alone is insufficient.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates and the dispatch sub-issues it spawns.

SOURCES MONITORED (every Monday — continue on per-source failure, log "skipped <source>: <reason>" to stdout):
- anthropics/claude-code — releases, /skills, /hooks, /subagents, /mcp directories
- anthropic-experimental/* — every public repo under that org (filter for "skill"/"agent")
- awesome-claude-code-toolkit
- awesome-claude-code-subagents
- awesome-agent-skills
- supabase/* — any repo with topic agent / skill / mcp
- hanoi-rainbow (https://github.com/hanoi-rainbow)
- MCP servers index (https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers + https://mcpservers.org)

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `gh` CLI where possible; fall back to `curl` ONLY against public GitHub raw URLs (never against Multica). For every CLI call use `--output json`. Continue on single-call failure.

(2) Per source, collect new or changed items in the window:
   - For repos: `gh api repos/<owner>/<name>/commits?since=<since>` and `gh api repos/<owner>/<name>/releases` filtered by published_at >= since.
   - For org listings (anthropic-experimental, supabase, hanoi-rainbow): `gh api orgs/<org>/repos?type=public&sort=pushed` and keep repos pushed in the window.
   - For awesome-* lists: diff the README at HEAD vs HEAD~7days (`gh api repos/<o>/<r>/contents/README.md` then compare). New bullet = new item.
   - For MCP servers index: diff the servers list (servers.json or README) the same way.
   Deduplicate by canonical URL. Cap the candidate set at 40 items per sweep — if more, keep the 40 with most-recent activity.

(3) Classify each candidate as MCP or non-MCP. MCP candidates include: anything with `modelcontextprotocol` in the URL or topics, anything named `mcp-*` or `*-mcp`, anything that ships a Model Context Protocol server. Everything else is non-MCP (skill / subagent / template).

(4) Evaluate each candidate:
   - MCP candidates → invoke the `mcp-server-evaluator` skill (depends on DEV-54). If that skill is not yet available, fall back to the lightweight inline checklist: (a) what protocol surface it exposes, (b) does it duplicate any tool we already have, (c) install footprint (deps / runtime / auth), (d) maintenance signal (last commit, open issues, stars), (e) any obvious security smell (writes to disk, requires broad OAuth scopes, third-party API exfil).
   - Non-MCP candidates → run the lightweight inline `skill-evaluator` rubric: (a) maps cleanly to a Multica skill shape (purpose, trigger, input, steps, output, quality checklist), (b) is it already covered by an existing skill (search `multica skill list --output json` for name overlap), (c) license check (MIT/Apache/BSD = green; AGPL/copyleft/none = needs decision), (d) maintenance signal, (e) security smell.

(5) Bucket each candidate into one of four labels and write the report. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue with this exact shape:

```

(repeat for each)

## Adapted (needs rework before import)

   - Gap: <what we'd change>
   - Effort: S | M | L

## Recreated as Multica skill (cleaner version)

   - Why recreate vs import: <reason>

## Rejected

   - Reason: <license | duplicate | unmaintained | security smell | scope mismatch>

## Top 3 imports → routed to skill-builder
- DEV-NN — <import #1>
- DEV-NN — <import #2>
- DEV-NN — <import #3>

## Next sweep window
<next-monday-10:00-Asia/Kuwait RFC3339>
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body in). Do NOT include any `mention://agent/...` link in this comment.

(6) Auto-dispatch the Top 3 imports as sub-issues. Selection rule: the 3 highest-confidence items in the "Imported" bucket (or fewer if fewer than 3 imports landed — never invent fillers). For each, create a sub-issue:

   - Title: "SKILL IMPORT: <name>"
   - Parent: this autopilot's issue id
   - Project: 09 - Research Intelligence (e977793d-b825-448a-a3f1-bb8f59cc1266)
   - Priority: high
   - Status: backlog initially (gate on security review — see below)
   - Assignee: skill-builder (5822179b-4e9e-40ea-bca2-70f4c80d2a19)
   - Description includes: source URL, license, what it does, proposed Multica skill name, PR-ready spec, Acceptance Criteria, DoD.

   SECURITY GATE — before flipping any dispatch sub-issue from `backlog → todo`, create one sibling security-review sub-issue assigned to security-auditor (17759d29-f9bb-4c4c-910c-b4476723db87) referencing the dispatch sub-issue. Title: "SECURITY REVIEW: <skill name> import". Body cites the source URL, install footprint, and required scopes from step (4). When the security-review sub-issue lands in `done` with `decision=green`, promote the dispatch sub-issue: `multica issue status <dispatch-id> todo`. If security review returns red, leave the dispatch sub-issue `backlog` and pin `blocked_reason=security_review_failed` to the dispatch sub-issue. Do NOT auto-import without the green stamp.

(7) Pin metadata on this issue ONLY when materially useful for future runs. Recommended keys:
   - `sweep_window_end` (RFC3339)

   - `imported_count` (number)

(8) Flip this issue's status to `in_review` so the CEO Command Squad can verify (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). Do NOT close it — CEO verifies week-over-week trend. Dispatch sub-issues stay `backlog` until security-auditor clears them, then auto-promote per step (6).

(9) Output to stdout (one line only, no comment): `GH Sweep @ <RFC3339> | sources=<covered>/8 | candidates=N | imported=N adapted=N recreated=N rejected=N | dispatched=N`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI/`gh`/HTTP failure. Log "skipped <source>: <reason>" and continue.
- Cap each sweep at 40 candidates total; cap dispatch at 3 sub-issues hard.
- Read metadata before pinning. Only update keys that changed since last sweep.

# /security-scan

## Name
security-scan

## Purpose
Security pre-merge scan — checks the diff for secrets, injection vulnerabilities, auth gaps, and data-access controls before code lands in production.

## Trigger
Agent task prompt starts with `/security-scan` (e.g. `/security-scan Scan PR #55 before merge`)

## Required Input
- The PR URL, branch name, or diff to scan
- The tech stack and framework (for targeted checks)

## Steps
1. **Secret detection** — scan the diff for:
   - Patterns: `password`, `api_key`, `secret`, `token`, `private_key`, `credentials` in variable names or string literals
   - File patterns: `.env`, `credentials.*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`
   - Base64 blobs that look like encoded secrets
   - Any hardcoded strings matching common API key formats (AWS, Stripe, etc.)
2. **Injection prevention**:
   - SQL injection: raw string interpolation into queries (no parameterized queries / ORM)
   - XSS: user-controlled data rendered via `innerHTML`, `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, or equivalent without sanitization
   - Command injection: user input passed to shell commands
3. **Auth and authorization**:
   - All new write routes (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) require authentication
   - All data-access queries scoped to the authenticated user (no `SELECT *` without user filter)
   - No privilege escalation: user cannot access another user's resources
4. **CSRF protection**: mutation routes use CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie policy.
5. **Input validation**: external input validated at system boundaries (API routes, webhooks, form handlers).
6. **RLS policies** (Supabase): every new user-scoped table has row-level-security enabled and policies defined; verify RLS is not bypassed with `service_role` key in client code.
7. **Dependency audit**: if new packages were added, flag any with known CVEs or unusual permissions.
8. Post a security gate comment: pass/fail per category with findings.

## Output
Security gate comment:
- Secrets: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- Injection: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- Auth / authz: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- CSRF: PASS / FAIL
- Input validation: PASS / FAIL
- RLS (if Supabase): PASS / FAIL / N/A
- Dependencies: PASS / FAIL / N/A
- **Gate: PASS / FAIL — merge recommended / blocked**

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Full diff reviewed (not just changed files at a glance)
- [ ] Secret patterns checked with regex, not just eyeballing
- [ ] Every new API route checked for auth middleware
- [ ] RLS checked for every new Supabase table
- [ ] Gate verdict stated explicitly (PASS = merge OK, FAIL = merge blocked)

## Failure Handling
- Diff not accessible: request the diff or branch name.
- Ambiguous finding (may or may not be a secret): flag as "manual review required" and do not block unless certain.
- Known false positive pattern: document why it was cleared.

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Add OWASP Top 10 checks incrementally. Retire specific checks if the CI pipeline adds automated equivalents (e.g. secret scanning via GitHub Advanced Security).

## Skill Registration

**Multica Skill ID:** `a1468c20-4dfa-423a-a54b-4a7e82a92100`
**Name:** `universal-connector-install`
**Version:** 1.1.0 — 2026-06-08 (security review additions from DEV-227)
**Source:** Original — devosnt Skill Factory (DEV-223)

---

## Purpose

Wire integrations into any Multica-built app using the modern **connector-as-app-install** pattern. Covers:

1. **Sub-skill A — OAuth Connector Install**: Stripe, Supabase, generic REST APIs via OAuth-grant. No API key ever in generated code.
2. **Sub-skill B — MCP Server Exposure**: Expose any Multica-built app as an MCP server for Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor — with explicit tool allowlist and API key auth.
3. **Sub-skill C — Connector Sidebar UX Spec**: Standard settings UI for surfacing, installing, and revoking connectors.

---

## Trigger Phrases

- "integrate Stripe / Supabase / [any third-party API]"
- "add payments", "add billing", "add subscriptions"
- "expose my app as an MCP server", "let Cursor use my app", "connect Claude Desktop to my app"
- "add a connector sidebar", "show available integrations"
- Build issue contains: *connector*, *OAuth integration*, *one-click install*, *MCP server*, *integration sidebar*

---

## Security Model

- OAuth tokens stored server-side only (never client bundle or git)
- MCP server scope is an explicit tool allowlist — no wildcard DB access
- MCP server auth via `MCP_SERVER_API_KEY` env var
- All data tools enforce RLS for multi-tenant isolation
- `secrets-leak-detector` required on every PR using this skill

---

## MCP Server Exposure — Security Requirements (Sub-skill B)

These requirements apply to every deployment generated from the MCP server exposure scaffold. They are non-negotiable and must be confirmed before any `in_review → done` transition on a build issue using this skill.

### 1. Input Validation (REQUIRED)
All MCP tool call arguments MUST be validated server-side before processing:
- Use Zod or equivalent schema validation on every tool handler
- Reject unknown/extra parameters
- Enforce maximum string lengths (default: 2048 chars unless the tool explicitly needs more)
- Validate enum values against an explicit allowlist
- Never pass raw user-supplied strings directly to DB queries or shell commands

### 2. Rate Limiting (REQUIRED — production blocker if missing)
The MCP server MUST be deployed behind a rate limiter:
- **Recommended:** max 60 tool calls/minute per API key
- **HTTP transport:** use Nginx `limit_req_zone` or equivalent reverse proxy rule
- **stdio/local deployment:** the host process (Claude Desktop, Cursor) is responsible for rate limiting; document this assumption explicitly in the app's README
- Without rate limiting, a misconfigured or abusive MCP client can exhaust server resources

### 3. Logging Policy (REQUIRED)
Log only what is needed for audit and incident response:
- **Log:** tool call name, timestamp, API key hash (first 8 characters only — never the full key)
- **Never log:** tool call arguments or return values — they may contain user PII or business-sensitive data
- Logs must be queryable by timestamp and API key hash for incident investigation
- No secrets or full tokens in any log line

### 4. HTTP Transport CORS (REQUIRED if HTTP transport is used)
The scaffold defaults to stdio transport, which has no CORS surface. If a deployment switches to HTTP transport:
- Set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` to an explicit origin allowlist — never `*`
- Never allow `*` for credentialed requests
- Document the allowed origins in the deployment's env var config

---

## Deployment Checklist (Sub-skill B)

Before any production deployment using the MCP server exposure scaffold, confirm all of the following:

- [ ] `MCP_SERVER_API_KEY` generated via `openssl rand -hex 32` and stored in secrets manager (not in git or env file)
- [ ] `ALLOWED_TOOLS` allowlist is explicit — no wildcard entries
- [ ] All tool handlers have Zod argument validation
- [ ] Rate limiter configured (60 calls/min/key or tighter)
- [ ] Logging configured: tool name + API key hash only
- [ ] HTTPS enforced if HTTP transport is used
- [ ] `secrets-leak-detector` passed on the PR diff
- [ ] RLS policies verified for all data tools
- [ ] `npm audit` run — no high/critical CVEs

---

## Source Issue

DEV-223 — BENCHMARK ACTION: Universal MCP/connector-as-app-install pattern

## Security Review

DEV-227 — Security Review: MCP server exposure scaffold (CONDITIONAL PASS — all gaps addressed in v1.1.0)

## Improvement Rule

After 3 uses: review requested connectors and update default list. After 6 uses: extract per-connector sub-skills.
issues.title: SKILL: universal-connector-install — OAuth connector + MCP server exposure

## Purpose
This issue was created to demonstrate the updated `user-stories-to-issues` scaffold (v2, Jun 2026) producing SEO checklist items automatically in the Acceptance Criteria section for a web app build task.

## Input
- User story: "As a visitor, I want to view a public product listing page so that I can browse available items before registering."
- Architecture: Next.js + Supabase, publicly accessible product catalog, authenticated checkout flow.
- Labels: `frontend`, `full-stack`

## Expected Output
A working product listing page at `/products` with server-side rendering, proper pagination, and search.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional
- [ ] `/products` page loads and renders all active product records from Supabase.
- [ ] Products are paginated (20 per page); pagination controls visible.
- [ ] Client-side search filters products by name in real time.
- [ ] Unauthenticated visitors can view the listing; add-to-cart requires auth (redirect to `/login`).
- [ ] Loading skeleton shown while data fetches; empty state shown when no products match.
- [ ] Error state shown (with retry) if the Supabase query fails.

### SEO (auto-injected — required for all web app issues)
- [ ] Semantic HTML: `<h1>`→`<h2>`→`<h3>` hierarchy correct; `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, `<section>`, `<footer>` used semantically.
- [ ] Meta title: unique per page, ≤60 characters, describes the page accurately.
- [ ] Meta description: unique per page, ≤160 characters, written for click-through.
- [ ] Open Graph tags: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url` present on every public-facing page.
- [ ] `robots.txt` at `/robots.txt`: allows crawlers on public routes; disallows `/dashboard`, `/admin`, and all auth-gated routes.
- [ ] `sitemap.xml` at `/sitemap.xml`: auto-generated, covers all public URLs, noted for Google Search Console submission.

## Dependencies
- Supabase `products` table and RLS policies must exist (see architecture issue).
- Auth redirect middleware must be in place.

## Risk
Low. Read-only data display; no write paths on the public listing page.

## Definition of Done
- `/products` renders in a browser with real data.
- All functional and SEO acceptance criteria checked off.
- QA has run `seo-audit-checklist` against the deployed preview URL and posted a PASS verdict.
- No secrets in the client bundle (grep verified).

# /qa-check

## Name
qa-check

## Purpose
QA checklist before merge — verifies that acceptance criteria, edge cases, UI states, validation, and security basics are all covered.

## Trigger
Agent task prompt starts with `/qa-check` (e.g. `/qa-check PR #42 — user auth flow`)

## Required Input
- The PR URL or diff to review
- The original acceptance criteria (from the issue or PR description)
- The feature under test (component, route, or flow)

## Steps
1. Read the issue acceptance criteria and PR diff together.
2. For each acceptance criterion, verify it is demonstrably met in the diff.
3. Check UI states:
   - Loading state present (spinner, skeleton, or placeholder)
   - Empty state present (helpful message, not blank)
   - Error state present (user-friendly message, no raw stack traces)
4. Check form validation:
   - Required fields validated client-side
   - Appropriate error messages shown inline
   - Server-side validation errors displayed to user
5. Check edge cases:
   - Empty inputs / null values handled
   - Long text does not break layout
   - Network failure / timeout handled gracefully
6. Check mobile responsiveness (375px viewport at minimum).
7. Verify no `console.error` or warning-level logs in the happy path.
8. Verify no hardcoded credentials, API keys, or tokens in the diff.
9. Post a QA pass/fail checklist as a comment on the issue.

## Output
QA checklist comment with pass/fail per criterion:
- Acceptance criteria: [AC1] pass | [AC2] fail — reason
- UI states: loading ✓ | empty ✓ | error ✓
- Form validation: ✓ / ✗
- Edge cases: ✓ / ✗
- Mobile responsive: ✓ / ✗
- No console errors: ✓ / ✗
- No hardcoded secrets: ✓ / ✗
- **Overall: PASS / FAIL**

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Every acceptance criterion addressed (pass or documented failure)
- [ ] All three UI states verified
- [ ] Form validation verified
- [ ] At least 3 edge cases checked
- [ ] Mobile layout verified
- [ ] Secret scan done
- [ ] Overall verdict (PASS/FAIL) stated clearly

## Failure Handling
- No acceptance criteria found: flag as "AC missing — QA blocked" and request them before proceeding.
- Cannot run the code: perform static analysis of the diff and note "runtime verification required."

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Add platform-specific checks (e.g. Supabase RLS, Arabic RTL layout) as the workspace's tech stack stabilizes.
